Code fragment: public final TableField<SourceSystemRecord, TranslatorType> TRANSLATOR_TYPE = createField(DSL.name("translator_type"), SQLDataType.VARCHAR.nullable(false).asEnumDataType(TranslatorType.class), this, "");
Explanation: Using an ENUM type for 'translator_type' embeds allowed values directly in the column definition, making it difficult to add or modify values without altering the schema. Consider creating a separate lookup table (e.g., 'translator_type') with a foreign key reference instead.

Code fragment: public final TableField<SourceSystemRecord, String> MAPPING_ID = createField(DSL.name("mapping_id"), SQLDataType.CLOB.nullable(false), this, "");
